Title: Create a provider linked to an existing person record
Preconditions: Logged in as a user with Manage Providers privilege; target person record exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open Administration > Manage Providers | 2. Click Add Provider | 3. Search and select the existing person | 4. Enter Identifier | 5. Save
Expected_Result: Provider is created, linked to the selected person, and appears in the provider list with the entered identifier

Title: Create a provider with name only (no linked person)
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Providers privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Open Manage Providers | 2. Click Add Provider | 3. Leave person blank | 4. Enter free-text provider name | 5. Enter identifier | 6. Save
Expected_Result: Provider is created without a person link and listed by the free-text name

Title: Reject create when neither person nor provider name is supplied
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Providers privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Open Add Provider | 2. Leave both person and provider name blank | 3. Enter identifier only | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked with a validation error requiring a person or a provider name; no record is created

Title: Reject duplicate provider identifier
Preconditions: An existing provider already uses identifier PRV-1001
Test_Steps: 1. Open Add Provider | 2. Select a different person | 3. Enter an identifier already in use | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Save is rejected with a duplicate identifier validation error; no second record is created

Title: Identifier field boundary - maximum length
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Providers privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Open Add Provider | 2. Select a person | 3. Enter identifier at the maximum allowed length | 4. Save | 5. Repeat with length+1
Expected_Result: At max length the provider saves; at max+1 the input is truncated or rejected with a length error

Title: Identifier accepts leading/trailing whitespace trimming
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Providers privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Open Add Provider | 2. Enter identifier with leading and trailing spaces | 3. Save | 4. Reopen provider and inspect stored identifier
Expected_Result: Identifier is stored trimmed; duplicate detection treats trimmed value consistently

Title: Create provider rejects script/HTML in name field (XSS)
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Providers privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Open Add Provider | 2. Enter a script payload as provider name | 3. Save | 4. View provider list and detail page
Expected_Result: Payload is rejected or stored encoded; no script executes when rendered in list or detail views

Title: SQL injection attempt in identifier search is neutralized
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Providers privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Open provider search | 2. Enter a SQL meta-character payload | 3. Submit search | 4. Observe results and server behavior
Expected_Result: Search returns a safe empty or literal-match result; no SQL error and no data loss

Title: Edit provider identifier and verify audit of change
Preconditions: An existing provider PRV-1001 is present; auditing enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Open Manage Providers | 2. Open provider PRV-1001 | 3. Change identifier | 4. Save | 5. Review audit/change log
Expected_Result: Identifier is updated, list reflects new value, and the change is captured in the audit trail with user and timestamp

Title: Re-link provider from one person to another
Preconditions: Provider linked to Person A exists; Person B exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the provider | 2. Change linked person from A to B | 3. Save | 4. Verify provider detail and prior encounters
Test_Data: Person A=Jane Doe; Person B=John Roe
Expected_Result: Provider is re-linked to Person B; historical encounters retain the provider reference without orphaning

Title: Concurrent edit conflict on the same provider
Preconditions: Two admin sessions open the same provider simultaneously
Test_Steps: 1. Session 1 edits identifier and saves | 2. Session 2 edits name using stale data and saves | 3. Observe outcome
Expected_Result: System prevents silent overwrite via optimistic locking error or last-write is auditable; no data corruption

Title: Link a provider to a login user account
Preconditions: Provider exists; a user account exists for the same person
Test_Steps: 1. Open the linked person's user account | 2. Verify provider association via person | 3. Create an encounter selecting that provider
Expected_Result: Provider and user resolve to the same person; encounter records the provider and the authoring user consistently

Title: Provider without user account cannot authenticate
Preconditions: Provider exists with no associated user account
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to log in using the provider identifier | 2. Observe result
Expected_Result: Login fails; provider records are not credentials and cannot be used to authenticate

Title: Assign a provider role to a provider
Preconditions: Provider Management module installed; provider roles configured
Test_Steps: 1. Open Manage Provider Roles | 2. Edit a provider | 3. Assign a provider role | 4. Save | 5. Verify on provider detail
Expected_Result: Provider role is assigned and visible on the provider detail and in role-based provider queries

Title: Provider role supervision hierarchy enforcement
Preconditions: Provider roles with supervisor/supervisee relationships configured
Test_Steps: 1. Configure role A supervises role B | 2. Assign provider P1 role A, P2 role B | 3. Attempt supervision link P1->P2 | 4. Attempt invalid link P2->P1
Expected_Result: Permitted supervision (A over B) succeeds; inverse or peer supervision is rejected per the role hierarchy rules

Title: Pairwise role and specialty combinations
Preconditions: Multiple provider roles and specialties configured
Test_Steps: 1. Create providers covering pairwise combinations of role x specialty x status | 2. Save each | 3. Filter providers by each axis
Expected_Result: Each pairwise combination saves and is correctly filterable; no combination produces an error or mis-categorization

Title: Add a custom provider attribute type and value
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Provider Attribute Types privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Open Manage Provider Attribute Types | 2. Create attribute type | 3. Edit a provider | 4. Set the attribute value | 5. Save
Expected_Result: Attribute type is created and the value is saved on the provider and shown on the detail page

Title: NPI attribute fails check-digit validation
Preconditions: NPI provider attribute type configured with format validation
Test_Steps: 1. Edit a provider | 2. Enter an NPI failing the Luhn check digit | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Save is rejected with an invalid NPI validation message; value is not persisted

Title: Required provider attribute enforced on save
Preconditions: A provider attribute type is marked required
Test_Steps: 1. Edit a provider | 2. Leave the required attribute blank | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked with a required-field error until the attribute is provided

Title: Date-typed attribute boundary - expired license date
Preconditions: A date-typed License Expiry attribute exists
Test_Steps: 1. Edit a provider | 2. Set License Expiry to yesterday | 3. Save | 4. Run providers-with-expired-license report
Expected_Result: Provider saves; expired-license report flags the provider as expired as of today 2026-07-01

Title: Create a provider weekly availability schedule
Preconditions: Appointment scheduling module enabled; provider exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open provider scheduling | 2. Define availability blocks for the week | 3. Save | 4. Open appointment booking for the provider
Expected_Result: Schedule is saved and bookable slots reflect the defined availability windows

Title: Overlapping availability blocks are rejected or merged
Preconditions: Provider has an existing availability block
Test_Steps: 1. Add a second block overlapping the first | 2. Save | 3. Inspect resulting schedule
Expected_Result: Overlap is rejected with an error or merged into a single 09:00-14:00 block per defined rule; no duplicate slots

Title: Schedule boundary at day rollover and timezone
Preconditions: Provider scheduling enabled; server timezone configured
Test_Steps: 1. Create an availability block crossing midnight | 2. Save | 3. View slots in two client timezones
Expected_Result: Cross-midnight block is handled without slot loss; slots render correctly per client timezone without off-by-one-day errors

Title: Booking blocked outside provider availability
Preconditions: Provider availability Mon-Fri 09:00-17:00 defined
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to book an appointment Saturday | 2. Attempt to book at 18:00 weekday | 3. Observe
Expected_Result: Both out-of-availability bookings are blocked with an unavailable-slot message

Title: Retire an active provider with a reason
Preconditions: Active provider PRV-1001 exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the provider | 2. Click Retire | 3. Enter a retire reason | 4. Save | 5. Verify list filtering
Expected_Result: Provider transitions Active->Retired, is hidden from default lists, and retains retiredBy/dateRetired/reason metadata

Title: Retire requires a non-empty reason
Preconditions: Active provider exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the provider | 2. Click Retire | 3. Leave reason blank | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Retire is blocked with a required-reason validation error; provider stays Active

Title: Unretire a retired provider
Preconditions: Provider is currently Retired
Test_Steps: 1. Filter to include retired providers | 2. Open the retired provider | 3. Click Unretire | 4. Save | 5. Verify default list
Expected_Result: Provider transitions Retired->Active, reappears in default lists, and retire metadata is cleared

Title: Retired provider not selectable for new encounters
Preconditions: Provider is Retired
Test_Steps: 1. Start a new encounter | 2. Open the provider picker | 3. Search for the retired provider
Expected_Result: Retired provider does not appear as a selectable option for new encounters; existing references remain intact

Title: Retire provider with active future appointments
Preconditions: Provider has future booked appointments
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to retire the provider | 2. Observe warning/blocking behavior | 3. Decide reassign vs cancel | 4. Confirm
Expected_Result: System warns about future appointments and requires reassignment or cancellation before retiring; no orphaned bookings

Title: Search provider by partial name
Preconditions: Multiple providers exist
Test_Steps: 1. Open Manage Providers | 2. Enter a partial name | 3. Submit search
Expected_Result: Matching providers (e.g., Jane) are returned case-insensitively ranked by relevance

Title: Search by identifier exact match
Preconditions: Provider PRV-1001 exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open provider search | 2. Enter the full identifier | 3. Submit
Expected_Result: Exactly the matching provider is returned

Title: Search excludes retired providers by default
Preconditions: One active and one retired provider share a name token
Test_Steps: 1. Search by the shared name token | 2. Note results | 3. Toggle include-retired | 4. Search again
Expected_Result: Default search returns only active matches; toggling include-retired adds the retired provider

Title: Search with no matches returns empty state
Preconditions: No provider matches the term
Test_Steps: 1. Search for a nonexistent token | 2. Observe
Expected_Result: An empty result/no-results message is shown without error

Title: Search handles unicode and diacritics
Preconditions: A provider named with diacritics exists
Test_Steps: 1. Create provider named Núñez | 2. Search using 'Nunez' and 'Núñez' | 3. Observe
Expected_Result: Both accented and unaccented searches locate the provider per normalization rules

Title: Provider list pagination boundary
Preconditions: More providers exist than one page
Test_Steps: 1. Open provider list | 2. Navigate to last page | 3. Verify count and no duplicates/gaps
Expected_Result: Pagination shows correct totals, last page has remainder, and no provider is duplicated or skipped

Title: Unprivileged user cannot create or edit providers
Preconditions: User without Manage Providers privilege is logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to Manage Providers URL directly | 2. Attempt Add Provider | 3. Attempt to POST a provider create via UI
Expected_Result: Access is denied with an authorization error; no provider is created or modified

Title: Provider edit action is audited with actor identity
Preconditions: Auditing enabled; privileged user logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Edit a provider attribute | 2. Save | 3. Inspect the audit log entry
Expected_Result: Audit log records the actor username, timestamp, before/after values for the change

Title: Read a Practitioner resource by id
Preconditions: FHIR module enabled; provider exists with FHIR mapping
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Practitioner/{uuid} | 2. Inspect the response
Expected_Result: HTTP 200 returns a valid FHIR R4 Practitioner with id, identifier, name, and active=true

Title: Search Practitioner by identifier system and value
Preconditions: FHIR module enabled; provider with NPI identifier exists
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Practitioner?identifier=system|value | 2. Inspect bundle
Expected_Result: Bundle returns matching Practitioner(s); identifier.system and value echo the requested NPI

Title: Retired provider maps to Practitioner.active=false
Preconditions: A retired provider with FHIR mapping exists
Test_Steps: 1. Retire the provider | 2. GET the Practitioner resource | 3. Inspect active flag
Expected_Result: Practitioner.active is false reflecting the retired state

Title: Create Practitioner via FHIR POST
Preconditions: FHIR write enabled; client authorized
Test_Steps: 1. POST a valid Practitioner resource | 2. Capture Location/id | 3. GET the created resource
Expected_Result: HTTP 201 with Location header; created Practitioner is retrievable and reflected as a provider

Title: Reject FHIR Practitioner with malformed identifier system
Preconditions: FHIR write enabled
Test_Steps: 1. POST a Practitioner with a non-URI identifier.system | 2. Observe response
Expected_Result: HTTP 400/422 with an OperationOutcome describing the invalid identifier system; resource not created

Title: PractitionerRole links Practitioner to specialty and location
Preconditions: FHIR PractitionerRole supported; provider and location exist
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/PractitionerRole?practitioner={uuid} | 2. Inspect role, specialty, location references
Expected_Result: PractitionerRole resources resolve practitioner, specialty (code), and location references correctly

Title: Unauthorized FHIR client cannot read Practitioner
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint requires authentication
Test_Steps: 1. GET Practitioner without/with invalid credentials | 2. Observe
Expected_Result: HTTP 401/403 returned; no Practitioner data is disclosed

Title: Practitioner.name follows HumanName structure
Preconditions: Provider with structured given/family name exists
Test_Steps: 1. GET the Practitioner | 2. Inspect name.family and name.given array
Expected_Result: name.family and name.given are populated correctly with text reflecting the full display name

Title: Inbound HL7 message resolves ordering provider via XCN
Preconditions: HL7 inbound processing enabled; provider mapped to HL7 id
Test_Steps: 1. Submit an ORM/ORU message with ORC-12 ordering provider | 2. Process | 3. Inspect resulting order provider
Expected_Result: Message processes and the order is attributed to the resolved provider via the XCN identifier

Title: Unknown HL7 provider id is queued/errored not silently dropped
Preconditions: HL7 inbound processing enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Submit a message with an unmapped ordering provider id | 2. Process | 3. Inspect error queue
Expected_Result: Message is rejected to the error queue with a provider-not-found reason; no silent assignment to a default provider

Title: Provider identifier uniqueness enforced at DB layer
Preconditions: DB access to the provider table; one provider exists
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt a direct INSERT with a duplicate identifier | 2. Observe constraint behavior
Expected_Result: Insert fails on a unique/business constraint or app-level guard; duplicate is not persisted

Title: Retire sets retired flag and metadata columns
Preconditions: Provider exists; DB read access
Test_Steps: 1. Retire a provider via UI | 2. Query the provider row | 3. Inspect retired, retired_by, date_retired, retire_reason
Expected_Result: retired=true with retired_by, date_retired, and retire_reason populated consistently with the UI action

Title: Re-linking person does not orphan encounter_provider rows
Preconditions: Provider with historical encounters exists; DB read access
Test_Steps: 1. Re-link provider to a new person via UI | 2. Query encounter_provider rows referencing the provider | 3. Verify integrity
Expected_Result: All encounter_provider references remain valid pointing to the provider record; no NULL/orphan rows

Title: Create provider via REST and verify representation
Preconditions: REST module enabled; authorized session/token
Test_Steps: 1. POST /ws/rest/v1/provider with person and identifier | 2. Capture uuid | 3. GET the provider
Expected_Result: HTTP 201 returns the created provider; GET returns matching uuid, person link, and identifier

Title: Retire provider via REST DELETE with reason param
Preconditions: REST module enabled; provider exists
Test_Steps: 1. DELETE /ws/rest/v1/provider/{uuid}?reason=... | 2. GET the provider including retired
Expected_Result: HTTP 204; subsequent GET shows retired=true with the supplied reason

Title: REST create provider rejects missing required fields
Preconditions: REST module enabled; authorized
Test_Steps: 1. POST a provider with neither person nor name | 2. Observe response
Expected_Result: HTTP 400 with a validation error; no provider created

Title: REST provider create requires authentication
Preconditions: REST module enabled
Test_Steps: 1. POST a provider with no/invalid credentials | 2. Observe
Expected_Result: HTTP 401; no provider created and no sensitive data leaked in the error

Title: Add Provider form is keyboard and screen-reader accessible
Preconditions: Add Provider page loaded; screen reader available
Test_Steps: 1. Tab through all fields | 2. Verify labels announced | 3. Trigger a validation error | 4. Verify error association
Expected_Result: All controls are reachable and operable by keyboard with associated labels; errors are announced and linked to fields (WCAG 2.1 AA)

Title: Provider list table has proper headers and focus order
Preconditions: Provider list rendered
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect table semantics | 2. Navigate with screen reader | 3. Verify sort controls announce state
Expected_Result: Table uses header cells with scope, focus order is logical, and sortable columns announce ascending/descending state

Title: Exploratory session on provider lifecycle edge cases
Preconditions: Test environment with seed providers; charter defined
Test_Steps: 1. Time-box 60 min | 2. Probe rapid retire/unretire cycles, special characters, deep-link tampering, stale sessions | 3. Log anomalies
Expected_Result: Defects and inconsistencies (state, validation, audit gaps) are documented with repro steps; no data corruption observed

Title: Provider specialty multi-select pairwise validation
Preconditions: Multi-valued specialty attribute configured
Test_Steps: 1. Assign combinations of multiple specialties | 2. Save | 3. Filter by each specialty
Expected_Result: Each provider appears under all of its assigned specialties when filtered; no specialty is dropped or duplicated

Title: State machine - invalid transition unretire on active provider
Preconditions: Provider is currently Active
Test_Steps: 1. Open an active provider | 2. Inspect available actions
Expected_Result: Unretire action is unavailable/disabled for an active provider; only Retire is offered

Title: Practitioner update via PUT preserves id and increments version
Preconditions: FHIR write enabled; existing Practitioner
Test_Steps: 1. GET Practitioner and note versionId | 2. PUT an updated name | 3. GET again
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; id is preserved, name updated, and meta.versionId increments per FHIR versioning
